summary: Matched the grdynamicattr translation unit by replacing asm/melee/gr/grdynamicattr.s with src/melee/gr/grdynamicattr.c and updating obj_files.mk to build the C object. The new C source reconstructs a small fixed-size pool/list manager for dynamic ground attributes, including initialization, allocation with an assert on floor_id, removal/free-list reinsertion, lifetime countdown, and a proximity query using sqrtf over X/Y distance after a floor-compatibility check.
what_changed: Added src/melee/gr/grdynamicattr.c with 116 lines of C matching the former assembly.
Changed obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/gr/grdynamicattr.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grdynamicattr.s.o.
Defined a local UnkStruct layout with next pointer, stored return/id field, Vec position, floor id, radius/distance threshold, lifetime/countdown field, and explicit padding to 0x24 bytes.
Recreated static storage corresponding to lbl_8049EFA0 as a four-element UnkStruct pool plus two static list-head pointers corresponding to lbl_804D6960 and lbl_804D6964.
Implemented func_801CA0B4 as initialization of active/free-list globals and construction of the four-entry free list.
Implemented func_801CA0F8 as allocation from the free list into the active list, copying a Vec3, storing floor_id, radius, and countdown, and asserting floor_id != GC_Id_None at source line 55.
Implemented func_801CA1C0 as removal from the active list followed by reinsertion into the free list, with a goto label used to match control flow.
Implemented func_801CA224 as an active-list update that decrements positive countdown values and frees entries whose countdown reaches zero.

Implemented func_801CA284 as a query over active entries that first calls func_80054F68(arg1, cur->unk14), then checks X/Y Euclidean distance against cur->unk18, returning cur->unk4 on match or 0 otherwise.
smart_moves: Used a fixed-size static array of four structs to match the original .bss allocation size of 0x90 bytes, with each struct padded to 0x24 bytes.
Kept unknown labels and function names where semantic names were not established, avoiding unsupported renaming while still matching the code.
Introduced typedef Vec Vec3 so the decompiled source could use the existing Dolphin vector layout while preserving a local naming style seen in the reconstruction.
Represented the two global pointers as active-list and free-list heads, which cleanly explains the assembly pattern of moving nodes between lbl_804D6960 and lbl_804D6964.
Used assert_line(55, floor_id!=GC_Id_None) to reproduce the original __assert call using the string grdynamicattr.c and condition floor_id!=GC_Id_None.
Used a small inline do_sqrtf wrapper around sqrtf, likely to preserve the exact floating-point codegen needed for the distance calculation.
Preserved matching-oriented control flow in func_801CA1C0 with a goto insert label rather than forcing a more idiomatic but potentially nonmatching list-removal structure.
Included an unused local array u32 unused[2] in func_801CA0F8, likely to control stack layout and match the original frame.
decomp_lessons: When a .bss blob is a fixed pool, derive struct size from the allocation size and observed pointer strides; here 0x90 bytes divided into four 0x24-byte entries.
List-head globals in small data can often represent an active list plus a free list; matching the pointer movement in assembly can make allocator-like code straightforward.
For decompilation matching, explicit padding fields such as x0_fill[0x24 - 0x20] are useful when field semantics are not fully known but struct size is clear.
Assert strings in assembly provide strong evidence for source filename, condition spelling, and assert line number; reproduce them with assert_line when available.
Distance calculations may require preserving sqrtf codegen details; a wrapper inline can help produce the same frsqrte/Newton iteration sequence as the target.
It is acceptable to leave names like UnkStruct, unk4, unk14, and unk1C when the PR evidence does not establish stronger semantic names.
When matching list removal, do not over-simplify control flow if the compiler needs a specific branch/goto structure.
Updating obj_files.mk from asm object to src object is the final integration step that marks the translation unit as matched.
naming_conventions: The new C file kept original placeholder symbol names for unidentified globals and functions: func_801CA0B4, func_801CA0F8, func_801CA1C0, func_801CA224, func_801CA284, lbl_8049EFA0, lbl_804D6960, and lbl_804D6964.
Unknown struct and field names used placeholder forms: UnkStruct, unk4, unk8, unk14, unk18, unk1C.
Padding was named x0_fill, reflecting an explicit filler field rather than a semantic member.
The local constant was named GC_Id_None and assigned -1, matching the assert condition floor_id!=GC_Id_None.
A typedef Vec Vec3 was introduced for local readability while relying on Dolphin's existing Vec layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the assembly source only after adding a matching C translation unit and switching obj_files.mk to the C object.
Matched the bss pool by defining static UnkStruct lbl_8049EFA0[4], corresponding to the removed .bss .skip 0x90.
Matched small-data globals by using static UnkStruct* list heads corresponding to lbl_804D6960 and lbl_804D6964.
Used assert_line(55, floor_id!=GC_Id_None) to match the __assert call with line 0x37 and the original condition string.
Used u32 unused[2] in func_801CA0F8 to influence stack layout.
Used goto insert in func_801CA1C0 to preserve branch structure for removing and freeing nodes.
Used inline f32 do_sqrtf(f32 x) { return sqrtf(x); } to match the emitted sqrt path in func_801CA284.
Computed dx, dy, dx2, and dy2 as separate locals before sqrtf, reflecting the assembly's explicit arithmetic sequence.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review recorded, but the provided excerpt contains no reviewer text or actionable feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that renamed grdynamicattr functions or UnkStruct fields.
Search for func_801CA0F8 callsites to infer semantic names for arg0, floor_id, radius, and lifetime/countdown.
Search for func_801CA284 callsites to determine what dynamic attribute id it returns and how arg1 is used with func_80054F68.
Look for other stage/ground modules using fixed-size pools and active/free list heads for comparable matching patterns.
Check whether GC_Id_None is defined elsewhere in the project and whether this local static const was later replaced by a shared enum or header.
